context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T20:23Z after continuing Homestead/Studio plugin-surface diagnostics. current_tasks: Pushed CI-green commit 5176f7c8 `feat(homestead): audit surface render mode`: Homestead writes `data-refarm-surface-render-mode` on surface wrappers (`wrapper`, `html`, `text`), emits `ui:surface_rendered` after successful plugin render hooks, `surface-inspector` reads `surfaceRenderMode`, and apps/dev `/surfaces` ledger shows `render: ...`. CI run 25185289679 succeeded.

Pushed CI-green commit 3a5f0bc5 `feat(homestead): report surface render failures`: render hook exceptions now mark the wrapper render mode as `failed`, emit `ui:surface_render_failed` with slot/surface metadata and errorMessage, and `surface-inspector` treats failures as mounted-surface graph change events so Studio ledgers refresh. CI run 25185875575 succeeded.

Pushed CI-green commit 851e3a0f `test(homestead): preserve executable stream surfaces`: Homestead test now proves an executable plugin-provided surface mounted in the `streams` slot survives while Homestead renders live stream observations into `[data-refarm-stream-panel]` in the same slot, with render mode `html`. CI run 25186642664 succeeded.

Pushed CI-green commit 164a3c23 `feat(app-dev): show failed surface diagnostics`: apps/dev `/surfaces` diagnostics now include trusted mounted fixture, trusted failing render fixture, and external untrusted rejected fixture. The page describes mount/fail/reject proof. `surface-diagnostics.test.ts`, apps/dev type-check, apps/dev build, project validate, and diff check passed locally. CI run 25187078792 succeeded.
Pushed docs commits e8e3a146 and 80aa57af documenting render diagnostics/failure semantics. Latest handoff checkpoint before this one was 3df57275.
Push hook for 164a3c23 had non-blocking local lint/unit timeouts, but local targeted tests/type-check/build passed before push and CI completed green.
Known generated artifact caveat occurred again after apps/dev build: `validations/sqlite-benchmark/browser/public/sqlite3-worker1.mjs` changed by inlining LANG. It was inspected and restored.
blockers: Disk remains 99% used on the host mount with about 9.1GB free. Stable; no cleanup needed now. Check before broad builds. Use `npm run clean:light --silent` only if generated artifacts grow meaningfully or free space drops near 2-3GB.

Builds can rewrite `validations/sqlite-benchmark/browser/public/sqlite3-worker1.mjs` by inlining the LANG expression. Continue to inspect and `git restore` unless intentionally working on that validation artifact.
Rust target dirs were previously cleaned. Avoid broad Rust commands unless Rust is touched; use scoped commands from AGENTS.md.
Known Vite browser externalization warnings for `node:fs/promises` and `node:path` remain non-blocking for apps/dev/apps/me builds.
Known moderate npm audit findings remain non-blocking: Astro, DOMPurify, PostCSS, uuid transitive chains; 0 high/critical in pre-push/CI checks.
CI duration is becoming noticeable: user wants to later evaluate GitHub Actions speedups, but only if improvements do not damage the Actions architecture/composability already built.
next_actions: First command after compaction: `git status --branch --short && df -h /workspaces/refarm && git log --oneline -12`.
If this handoff is committed, push it and watch CI. It should be a project-only change and skip most heavy jobs.

Next best atomic slice: decide whether to formalize `renderHomesteadSurface` in plugin-manifest docs as a Homestead host convention, while keeping manifest execution-neutral and `ui:panel:render` as the capability gate.

Next daily-driver slice: evolve `renderStudioStreamSurfaceDemo(...)` from a static panel toward a host-owned surface context/action contract. Keep app behavior in apps/dev and Homestead primitives semantic; do not put app-specific actions in Homestead or DS.

Next diagnostics slice: surface-ledger could show failed render error details if useful, but keep presenter logic in apps/dev and Homestead `surface-inspector` read-only/semantic. Current table already shows `render: failed` from DOM metadata.

Potential CI-speed follow-up: inspect recent workflow timings and changed-area filters before changing YAML. Candidate improvements should preserve pinned-action/reusable/local-action architecture, avoid broad reordering, and be backed by run timing evidence.

If future apps/social starts, consume `bootStudioRuntime(...)`, `setupStudioShell(...)`, `createStudioPluginHandle(...)`/`createHomesteadSurfacePluginHandle(...)`, and renderer-only subpaths rather than importing the full Homestead SDK in app internals.
open_questions: Should `renderHomesteadSurface` become a formal manifest-declared API/capability beyond the current `ui:panel:render` capability, or is the method convention enough until more real plugins exercise it?

Should plugin-provided `{ html }` be sanitized or restricted further before broader external plugin enablement? Current gate relies on Homestead trust activation; plain string/`{ text }` remains safe text path; failed render hooks are now auditable.
Should `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/surface-renderer` be documented in plugin-manifest README alongside `surface-inspector` as preferred narrow imports for app internals?

Should the Studio stream surface eventually consume live stream state directly via a host callback/context, or should Homestead remain the sole stream observer and executable surfaces only render static plugin affordances initially?
Do apps/dev and apps/me need shared TS path generation for Homestead subpaths to avoid manual path-map drift?
Which GitHub Actions jobs dominate runtime after changed-area filtering: quality verify, build, e2e, audit, or metrics? Gather evidence before tuning.
key_decisions_pending: Whether to formalize `renderHomesteadSurface` in plugin-manifest docs now or wait for one more executable plugin surface/failure fixture.
Whether next lane should be plugin-manifest docs, first host-owned action/context contract, or a small ledger error-detail presenter.
Whether to introduce a centralized app tsconfig path preset for Homestead subpaths.
Whether to schedule cleanup soon; current disk is stable, so no cleanup was run.
Whether/how to speed up GitHub Actions without weakening the composable architecture.
